text:
All eyes in the sporting world this summer will be on France for the upcoming 2024 Olympics in Paris.

While men's and women's teams from around the globe will battle for the traditional gold medal in team basketball, hoopers around the world will also be competing for a spot on the podium in 3x3 basketball for the second time in the history of the Olympics.

Like all Olympic sports, the television scheduleâwith both live and delayed broadcastsâcan be difficult to track. Here's everything you need to know about 3x3 Olympic basketball this summer.

If you are interested in checking out the five-on-five Olympic basketball schedule,follow this link.

3x3 basketball was featured at several youth Olympic games over the years, but it wasn't until 2021 when it made its debut at the Tokyo Olympics.

Latvia's 3x3 men's basketball team won gold in Tokyo, defeating the Russian Olympic Committee in the final. Serbia earned the bronze medal. On the women's side, Team USAâled by Kelsey Plum, Jackie Young, Allisha Gray and Stefanie Dolsonâwon the gold medal, beating out the ROC (silver) and China (bronze).

This summer in Paris, Hailey Van Lith, Cierra Burdick, Rhyne Howard and Dearica Hamby will represent Team USA in the women's tournament. (Hamby was added lateas an injury replacementfor Cameron Brink.) Jimmer Fredette, Dylan Travis, Kareem Maddox and Canyon Barry will play for Team USA on the men's side.

A game of 3x3 basketball is played on a half court with both teamsâmade up of four players (three on the court and one reserve)âdefending and attacking the same hoop. To win, a team must have the most points after 10 minutes of play or be the first team to reach 21 points. A shot beyond the perimeter is worth two points in 3x3 basketball, while a basket inside the arc is worth one point.

Eight teams will compete for the gold medal in both the men's and women's 3x3 basketball tournament. All teams will play seven games, matching up against the other countries in the pool play round. Teams will then be ranked based on their records.

After pool play, the top two teams advance to the semifinals, while the bottom two teams are eliminated. The remaining four teams will compete in a play-in round (No. 3 seed vs. No. 6 seed; No. 4 seed vs. No. 5 seed) to determine the other two semifinalists.

The Olympics will host every 3x3 matchup for both the men's and women's tournaments at La Concorde, an open arena located in the heart of the city.

The 2024 Olympics will be broadcast live by NBC and its other properties, including USA Network, CNBC, E! and the streaming platform Peacock. Every 3x3 basketball gameâfrom the round robin part of the tournament to the gold medal gameâwill be streamed live on Peacock, but NBC will also air games live over local NBC affiliates as well as E!, CNBC and USA Network.

While every 3x3 basketball game will be streamed live on Peacockâand some will be live on air on NBC, USA, E! and CNBCâthere will be tape-delayed coverage of the tournament throughout the Olympic Games.

Encore coverage of the 3x3 basketball tournament will be offered throughout the Olympics on NBC, USA Network and E!.

TheNBCUniversal networksthat will be broadcasting the Olympic Games in Paris are available on most cable packages. Ensure your cable package has access to these networks before the opening ceremony on July 26.

Every 3x3 basketball game will be streamed live on Peacock, NBC's exclusive streaming service. But there are other ways to stream the Olympic Games as well.

At this time, the service is not offering a free trial.

In order to sign up for Peacock, interested users must navigate to peacocktv.com and click "Get Started." Once there, Peacock offers two subscription packages to sign up forâannual and monthly. The monthly package costs $5.99 per month, and the annual costs $59.99 per year.

YoutubeTV has become one of the most popular alternative offerings. By signing up, users can stream their local NBC affiliate via the YouTubeTV app on their television or on their computer.

YouTubeTV'sÂbase plan costs $72.99Â per month and comes with NBC, USA, and E!. There is currently a five-day trial available.

title: Olympic 3x3 Basketball TV Schedule: Full List of Times, Channels, Live vs. Delay & More
description: Here's everything you need to know about the men's and women's Olympic 3x3 basketball tournaments.
